title: C8-MT031-SingleInstallerBuildSmoke

scope.summary:
Goal and expected diff shape:
Prove the CX-008-VIS single-installer guarantee: one installer artifact bundles ALL Handshake dependencies; a clean Windows user profile can install and launch the app with ZERO external requirements (no system WebView2, no separate PostgreSQL install, no CDN, no other app prerequisite). The diff: (1) `tests/native_gui/installer_build_smoke.rs` — Rust integration test that builds the installer artifact and runs a scripted install + launch smoke on a clean-profile Windows sandbox or a clean user-data directory; (2) `tests/native_gui/bundled_deps_audit.rs` — audit test that inspects the built installer artifact and asserts that all required DLLs, fonts, syntax grammars, PG binaries, and runtime assets are present inside the bundle; (3) `scripts/build_installer.ps1` — PowerShell 7 script that runs `cargo build --release`, packages the binary and all bundled assets, and produces a single installer artifact (WiX `.msi` or NSIS `.exe` or a self-extracting `.zip` depending on the toolchain available); (4) `src/frontend/installer/mod.rs` — a Rust module that at startup verifies the bundle is self-contained (checks for expected bundled asset paths relative to the exe) and reports any missing assets as a recoverable error with a clear user-facing message. No webview, no CDN, no external download at install time.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `tests/native_gui/installer_build_smoke.rs` — the installer smoke proof. Steps: (a) call `scripts/build_installer.ps1` (or `cargo build --release` + a packaging step) via `std::process::Command`; (b) assert the output artifact exists at `<workspace_root>/target/release/installer/handshake-setup.{msi|exe|zip}` and its file size is >= 10 MB (a rough lower-bound proving it is not a stub); (c) extract or install to a temp directory that represents a "clean profile" (no pre-existing Handshake data, no HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT env var set); (d) run the installed binary with `--self-check` flag which runs `installer::check_bundle_integrity()` and exits with code 0 (all assets present) or non-zero (missing asset); (e) assert exit code 0; (f) assert that the binary does NOT import `WebView2Loader.dll` from the system path (check with `dumpbin /dependents` or PowerShell `Get-Item` + `[System.Diagnostics.FileVersionInfo]`, or parse the PE import table with the `object` crate); (g) write a `BundleSmokeReport { artifact_path, artifact_size_bytes, self_check_exit_code, missing_assets: [], webview2_system_import: false }` to `tests/native_gui/artifacts/installer_smoke_report.json`.
- CREATE `tests/native_gui/bundled_deps_audit.rs` — static bundle audit. After the build step, walk the installer artifact (extracted to temp dir) and assert presence of: Handshake binary (`handshake-native.exe`); bundled PostgreSQL binaries in `bundled/postgres/` (from handshake_core's managed postgres feature); fonts directory with at least one `.ttf` or `.otf` file; syntax grammar files directory (tree-sitter grammars, at minimum for Rust, JS, Python); wgpu/vulkan/dx12 runtime DLLs if any are bundled (or assert they are statically linked); NO file named `WebView2Loader.dll` at the system-path level (a WebView2 DLL is only allowed inside the dedicated in-app browser pane subdirectory if that pane is included, not as a system dep). Write a `BundleDepsReport { files_found: [...], missing: [], disallowed_system_webview: false }` to `tests/native_gui/artifacts/bundle_deps_audit_report.json`.
- CREATE `scripts/build_installer.ps1` — PowerShell 7 build+package script. Runs `cargo build --release -p handshake-native`. Copies binary + all bundled assets (postgres binaries, fonts, grammars, wgpu shader cache, any embedded model runtime) to `target/release/staging/`. Either (a) invokes WiX 4 `wix build` to produce `handshake-setup.msi`, or (b) invokes NSIS `makensis` to produce `handshake-setup.exe`, or (c) produces `handshake-setup.zip` as a fallback if neither WiX nor NSIS is available. Sets `HANDSHAKE_INSTALLER_ARTIFACT` env var to the output path. Script exits non-zero on any failure. Must be disk-agnostic (no hardcoded drive letters — use `$PSScriptRoot` and `$env:CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`).
- CREATE `src/frontend/installer/mod.rs` — bundle integrity checker. `pub fn check_bundle_integrity() -> Result<(), BundleIntegrityError>` walks paths relative to `std::env::current_exe()?.parent()` and asserts presence of: `bundled/postgres/pg_ctl.exe` (or platform equivalent), `fonts/` directory with >= 1 font file, `grammars/` directory, `handshake-native.exe` itself. Returns `BundleIntegrityError::MissingAsset { path }` for the first missing item. Called from main with `--self-check` flag (add to `src/frontend/main.rs` arg parsing).
- EDIT `src/frontend/main.rs` — add `--self-check` flag: if present, call `installer::check_bundle_integrity()`, print JSON result, and `std::process::exit(0)` on success or `std::process::exit(1)` on error.
- EDIT `Cargo.toml` (handshake-native) — add `object` crate as dev-dependency (for PE import-table inspection in the audit test, if needed). Add WiX / NSIS as optional build-time feature flags or document them as CI prerequisites.

Exact crates/functions/APIs/React-reference-files to use:
- `object` crate (dev-dep): `object::read::pe::PeFile64`, `import_table()`, `import_thunks()` — for parsing the PE import table to verify no system WebView2Loader.dll dependency in the release binary.
- `zip` crate (dev-dep, already likely in Cargo.lock): `zip::ZipArchive` — for inspecting a zip-based installer artifact.
- `handshake_core::managed_postgres` or equivalent — the managed PostgreSQL binary path used at runtime. Source: `src/backend/handshake_core/src/managed_postgres.rs`. The bundled postgres binaries must live at the path this module expects relative to the executable.
- `app/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` — reference for the existing Tauri bundle configuration: `bundle.resources: ["resources/"]` and icon paths. The native installer replicates this bundling strategy without Tauri, using direct file copying in `build_installer.ps1`.
- React reference for the clean-profile smoke concept: `app/tests/build_*.ts` scripts and `app/tests/*.spec.ts` — the existing Playwright harnesses already test offline bundling (e.g., `rich_editor_harness.tsx` cuts network and asserts zero CDN requests). The native installer smoke is the Rust equivalent: cut the system and assert zero external deps at launch.
- `src/backend/handshake_core/src/managed_postgres.rs` — check bundled postgres discovery path (relative to exe). The installer must place postgres binaries where this module looks for them.
- No React file directly ports to this MT — this is a new installer/build surface with no React equivalent. The closest reference is the existing Tauri `tauri.conf.json` bundle config and the `app/src-tauri/build.rs` resource copy logic.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Understand the bundled-postgres path contract: open `src/backend/handshake_core/src/managed_postgres.rs` and identify the path it resolves relative to `std::env::current_exe()`. Match that path in both the `build_installer.ps1` staging step and `installer::check_bundle_integrity()`.
2. In `scripts/build_installer.ps1`: use `$PSScriptRoot` to locate the workspace root. Run `cargo build --release`. Use `Get-ChildItem -Recurse` on `target/release/` to find the binary. Create `target/release/staging/` and copy: binary, `bundled/` (postgres), `fonts/` (all .ttf/.otf from `src/frontend/assets/fonts/`), `grammars/` (tree-sitter wasm or native grammars). Then attempt WiX: `wix --version` — if available run `wix build handshake.wxs -o target/release/installer/handshake-setup.msi`. Else attempt NSIS: `makensis --version` — if available run `makensis handshake.nsi`. Else create a zip: `Compress-Archive -Path staging/* -DestinationPath target/release/installer/handshake-setup.zip`. Write `$env:HANDSHAKE_INSTALLER_ARTIFACT` to the produced path.
3. In `tests/native_gui/installer_build_smoke.rs`: use `std::process::Command::new("pwsh")` with args `["-NoProfile", "-File", "scripts/build_installer.ps1"]`. Assert exit 0. Read `HANDSHAKE_INSTALLER_ARTIFACT` env var (or hardcode the default path `target/release/installer/handshake-setup.*` via glob). Assert file exists and size >= 10 MB. For the `--self-check` step: if the artifact is a zip, extract to `tempfile::tempdir()`, find `handshake-native.exe`, run it with `--self-check`. If MSI, skip the launch step (CI may not have admin rights to install MSI) and instead extract with `msiexec /a` or the `msi` crate. Assert `--self-check` exits 0. For the WebView2 check: use the `object` crate to parse the PE import table of `handshake-native.exe` and assert `WebView2Loader.dll` does NOT appear in the imports.
4. In `tests/native_gui/bundled_deps_audit.rs`: after build, walk the staging dir (or extracted zip dir). Assert: `handshake-native.exe` exists; `bundled/postgres/pg_ctl.exe` exists (or the platform-appropriate postgres binary name — check managed_postgres.rs for the exact filename); `fonts/` contains >= 1 font file; `grammars/` exists (even if empty for the first pass — assert it exists so future grammar additions don't break the bundle). Assert no `WebView2Loader.dll` at the top level or in `bundled/` (a WebView2 DLL IS allowed inside `bundled/browser_pane/` if that pane bundles its own WV2, but not as a system dep).
5. In `src/frontend/installer/mod.rs`: implement `check_bundle_integrity` with a clear `BundleIntegrityError` enum. Return a machine-readable JSON on `--self-check`: `{"status": "ok", "checked_paths": [...]}` or `{"status": "missing_asset", "path": "<rel_path>"}`. This is consumed by the smoke test.
6. The `--self-check` flag must not start the full app (no egui event loop, no PG connection). It only checks path existence and exits. This makes it safe to run in a minimal CI environment.
7. Disk-agnostic paths: NEVER use absolute paths in `build_installer.ps1` or `installer/mod.rs`. All paths are relative to `$PSScriptRoot` or `std::env::current_exe().parent()`. This satisfies GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-004.
8. CI prerequisites documentation: add a comment block at the top of `build_installer.ps1` listing required tools: Rust stable toolchain, `cargo` in PATH, optional WiX 4 (`winget install WiX.WiX` or `cargo install wix`), optional NSIS. Also add to `tests/native_gui/README.md` (create if missing) a one-paragraph description of the installer smoke prerequisites.
9. The `object` crate version: check `Cargo.lock` for an existing `object` dependency (likely pulled in by `gimli`/`addr2line` for debug info). If it exists, use that version. Otherwise add `object = { version = "0.36", features = ["read", "pe"] }` as a dev-dep.
10. WiX `handshake.wxs` template: create a minimal `src/frontend/installer/handshake.wxs` with placeholders for version, binary path, and bundled asset dirs. This does not need to be production-quality — it only needs to produce a valid MSI that the smoke can inspect. Full installer polish is a later work packet.
acceptance_criteria: AC-031-01: `pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/build_installer.ps1` exits 0 and produces an artifact at `target/release/installer/handshake-setup.{msi|exe|zip}` with file size >= 10 MB.

AC-031-02: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test installer_build_smoke -- --nocapture` exits 0 on Windows. `installer_smoke_report.json` contains `self_check_exit_code: 0` and `webview2_system_import: false` and `missing_assets: []`.
AC-031-03: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test bundled_deps_audit -- --nocapture` exits 0. `bundle_deps_audit_report.json` contains `missing: []` and `disallowed_system_webview: false`.

AC-031-04: The `--self-check` flag on the installed binary exits 0 on a temp directory with no HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT env var and no pre-existing Handshake data — proves clean-profile launch capability.
AC-031-05: Inspecting the release binary PE import table (via `object` crate or `dumpbin /dependents`) confirms `WebView2Loader.dll` does NOT appear in the system-level imports.
AC-031-06: The staging directory contains all required assets: `handshake-native.exe`, `bundled/postgres/pg_ctl.exe` (or platform equivalent), `fonts/` with >= 1 font file, `grammars/` directory.

AC-031-07: `scripts/build_installer.ps1` contains no hardcoded absolute paths (no drive letters, no `C:\`, no `D:\`). Verified by `rg -n '[A-Z]:\\' scripts/build_installer.ps1` returning zero matches.

AC-031-08: `installer::check_bundle_integrity()` returns `Ok(())` when called with the correct staging directory and `Err(BundleIntegrityError::MissingAsset { path })` when a required asset is removed — verified by a unit test in `src/frontend/installer/mod.rs`.
AC-031-09: `installer_smoke_report.json` and `bundle_deps_audit_report.json` are valid JSON parseable by `serde_json::from_str`.
proof_targets: PT-031-01: Run `pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/build_installer.ps1` and show the final line of output including the artifact path and file size.

PT-031-02: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native --test installer_build_smoke -- --nocapture` and paste the content of `tests/native_gui/artifacts/installer_smoke_report.json` showing `self_check_exit_code: 0`, `webview2_system_import: false`, `missing_assets: []`.

PT-031-03: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native --test bundled_deps_audit -- --nocapture` and paste the content of `tests/native_gui/artifacts/bundle_deps_audit_report.json` showing `missing: []`, `disallowed_system_webview: false`, and listing `files_found` including the postgres binary and font file.
PT-031-04: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native installer check_bundle_integrity_returns_ok` (unit test inside installer/mod.rs) and show exit 0.
PT-031-05: Run `rg -n '[A-Z]:\\' scripts/build_installer.ps1` and show zero matches (no hardcoded absolute paths).
implementation_notes: The managed PostgreSQL binary path: `handshake_core::managed_postgres` resolves postgres binaries relative to the runtime root, NOT relative to the exe. Check `src/backend/handshake_core/src/managed_postgres.rs` for the exact discovery logic. The installer must place postgres binaries where `managed_postgres` expects them. If managed_postgres uses `HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT/bundled/postgres/`, then `build_installer.ps1` must stage them there. If it uses `exe_dir/bundled/postgres/`, stage them there. Do NOT guess — read the source first.

For the WebView2 check using the `object` crate: `let data = std::fs::read(exe_path)?; let file = object::File::parse(&*data)?;` then for PE files `let imports = file.imports()?; for import in imports { if import.library().eq_ignore_ascii_case(b"webview2loader.dll") { fail } }`.

WiX 4 can be installed via `cargo install wix` (the `cargo-wix` crate). The `scripts/build_installer.ps1` should try `cargo wix --nocapture` first (which handles WXS generation automatically for simple crates), falling back to manual `wix build handshake.wxs`. The `handshake.wxs` template only needs to package the staging directory — no custom actions, no registry writes beyond the standard uninstall key.

NSIS: if WiX is unavailable, `makensis` must be in PATH. The NSIS script `src/frontend/installer/handshake.nsi` should be a minimal InstallDir + CopyFiles + CreateShortcut script. The smoke only checks the file is produced with the right size — not that the MSI/NSIS installer actually runs in a GUI.
Zip fallback: `Compress-Archive` is built into PowerShell. The zip fallback is always available. Use it as the final fallback so the smoke can always complete on any CI machine.

Clean-profile test: a true clean Windows profile test requires a VM snapshot or Windows Sandbox. For CI, simulate a clean profile by: (a) using a temp directory with no Handshake data, (b) clearing `HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT` and `HANDSHAKE_RUNTIME_ROOT` env vars, (c) running `--self-check`. This is not identical to a clean-profile VM but is the best practical automated approximation without a full VM setup. Document this limitation in `tests/native_gui/README.md`.

Font bundling: the egui text rendering (cosmic-text or egui's built-in font system) requires fonts to be bundled. Identify which fonts are used in MT-003 (theme token system) and copy them from `src/frontend/assets/fonts/` to `staging/fonts/`. If no fonts have been added yet, bundle at minimum a single open-source monospace font (e.g., JetBrains Mono Nerd Font or Fira Mono) as a placeholder.

Grammar bundling: tree-sitter grammars may be compiled at build time (via `build.rs`) or loaded at runtime from `.wasm` files. Check `src/frontend/` for any `build.rs` or grammar loading code to determine the expected layout. If no grammar loading has been implemented yet, create the `grammars/` directory in staging as an empty placeholder — `bundled_deps_audit` only asserts the directory exists.

The `object` crate version: run `cargo tree -p handshake-native --edges dev 2>/dev/null | grep object` to find if `object` is already a transitive dep. If yes, use that version. Else add `object = "0.36"` with features `["read", "pe"]`.
PE import table is only available on Windows targets. Gate the WebView2 import check with `#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]`. On Linux CI, skip that assertion and document the skip.

Report artifact dir for this MT: same as MT-029 and MT-030 — `tests/native_gui/artifacts/`. All three MTs share this artifact dir. Filenames are distinct: `installer_smoke_report.json`, `bundle_deps_audit_report.json`.
ports_from_react: app/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json — reference for bundle configuration (resources list, icon paths, targets). The native installer replicates the same asset manifest without Tauri.
app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs and build.rs — reference for how Tauri bundles resources at build time. `build_installer.ps1` replaces this with an explicit PowerShell staging step.

app/src/harness/rich_editor_harness.tsx (pattern only) — the offline proof with network cut is the conceptual equivalent of the clean-profile install smoke (no external deps allowed). The native equivalent cuts the system instead of the network.
No single React file ports directly to this MT — installer tooling is entirely new in the native shell.
binds_backend_api: src/backend/handshake_core/src/managed_postgres.rs — read to determine the expected bundled postgres binary path relative to the runtime root or exe dir. The installer staging step MUST match this path contract.

handshake_core bundle feature (if any) — check Cargo.toml for a `bundle-postgres` or `managed-postgres` feature flag that triggers postgres binary inclusion in the build. Enable it for the release build in `build_installer.ps1`.
No direct backend HTTP API calls — this MT is build/packaging tooling only.

hbr_obligations: HBR-INT: the installer build smoke and bundled-deps audit are integration-level proof gates. CI must run both before WP-KERNEL-011 can be closed. Artifact reports are the formal evidence.

HBR-MAN: the WiX/NSIS installer tooling requires a human-reviewable manifest (handshake.wxs or handshake.nsi) that lists every bundled component. The manifest must be committed and reviewed before the installer ships.

HBR-STOP: if `--self-check` exits non-zero (missing asset detected at launch), the app must NOT start. This is a hard stop: the binary must exit with a clear error message listing the missing assets rather than partially initialising.
HBR-VIS: the `installer_smoke_report.json` and `bundle_deps_audit_report.json` are machine-readable evidence files that model agents and CI can inspect without reading build logs.

risks: RISK-031-01: The managed_postgres binary path contract may differ from what `build_installer.ps1` stages. Mitigation: MUST read `managed_postgres.rs` before writing the staging step. Assert the path in `bundled_deps_audit.rs` using the exact constant/path from that module.

RISK-031-02: WiX 4 and NSIS may both be unavailable on the CI runner. Mitigation: zip fallback is always available; smoke only requires the artifact to exist and be >= 10 MB. Real installer tooling (WiX/NSIS) is a nice-to-have for the first pass.

RISK-031-03: The `object` crate PE import table check may miss WebView2 imports if they are loaded via `LoadLibraryW` at runtime (delay-loaded). Mitigation: also check for `WebView2Loader.dll` as a file in the staging directory (not just in static imports) via bundled_deps_audit.

RISK-031-04: Font bundling may be omitted if no fonts have been added in MT-003. Mitigation: create a minimal font bundle with one open-source font as a build-time prerequisite. The smoke only asserts the `fonts/` directory is non-empty.

RISK-031-05: The installer artifact may be produced on a developer machine but fail on a clean CI runner due to missing build prerequisites (Rust toolchain, WiX, NSIS). Mitigation: `build_installer.ps1` must check for each prerequisite and emit a clear error message. Document prerequisites in `tests/native_gui/README.md`.

RISK-031-06: `--self-check` on a clean profile may fail because managed_postgres cannot find its binaries if the staging layout is wrong. Mitigation: the unit test for `check_bundle_integrity` creates a minimal staging tree in tempdir and verifies the check passes — catches layout bugs before the full smoke runs.

RISK-031-07: Hardcoded absolute paths accidentally introduced by a coder in `build_installer.ps1` (common mistake on Windows). Mitigation: AC-031-07 explicitly runs `rg '[A-Z]:\\' scripts/build_installer.ps1` and requires zero matches.

RISK-031-08: The installer file size lower bound of 10 MB may be wrong if postgres binaries are not yet bundled. Mitigation: document that the 10 MB bound is post-postgres-bundling; if postgres bundling is deferred, lower the bound to 5 MB and add a TODO note in the smoke.
minimum_controls: CTRL-031-01: `pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/build_installer.ps1` must exit 0 in CI before MT-031 can be marked VALIDATOR_PASS.
CTRL-031-02: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test installer_build_smoke` must exit 0 with `self_check_exit_code: 0` and `webview2_system_import: false` in the report.
CTRL-031-03: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test bundled_deps_audit` must exit 0 with `missing: []` in the report.
CTRL-031-04: `rg -n '[A-Z]:\\' scripts/build_installer.ps1` must return zero matches — no hardcoded drive letters.

CTRL-031-05: The `--self-check` command must exit non-zero if any required asset is missing — verified by the unit test in `src/frontend/installer/mod.rs` that removes a staging asset and asserts `Err(BundleIntegrityError::MissingAsset { .. })`.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the installer build/stage/package, runtime exe-relative bundle self-check, clean-profile launch smoke, real-PE WebView2 import check, and bundled-deps audit are all real (genuine release-native crt-static binary built, staged, launched, and object-crate PE-parsed; real zip artifact; real exe-relative bundled-postgres discovery in managed_postgres.rs), and the sole un-met literal bound (>=10MB compressed) is the contract's OWN RISK-031-08 postgres-deferred fallback, honestly recorded with postgres_deferred:true in the machine-readable report.
code_examined: D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/wtc-worksurface-shell-v1/src/frontend/handshake_native/assets/fonts/ (directory listing: Inter-Regular.ttf, Inter-Bold.ttf, OFL.txt)

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-01: build_installer.ps1 exits 0 and produces artifact >= 10 MB
acceptance_summary.evidence: build_installer.ps1:79 runs a real `cargo build --profile release-native --bin handshake-native`, stages exe+fonts+grammars+bundled/postgres (lines 99-148), and Compress-Archives a real single zip at <target>/release-native/installer/handshake-setup.zip (line 187). The LITERAL >=10MB COMPRESSED bound is NOT met (~6.47MB) because no postgres toolchain on this host -> documented placeholder pg_ctl (ps1:139-147). This is the contract's OWN RISK-031-08 fallback (assert staging-uncompressed >=10MB AND artifact >=5MB), asserted at installer_build_smoke.rs:194-202 and recorded with postgres_deferred:true + literal_ac_031_01_met flag in installer_smoke_report.json (smoke:282-292). Contract-sanctioned deferral, not a stub. Independently re-read both files; concur with first reviewer.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-02: installer_build_smoke exits 0; report self_check_exit_code:0, webview2_system_import:false, missing_assets:[]
acceptance_summary.evidence: installer_build_smoke.rs spawns real pwsh build (lines 125-146), locates the real artifact via the script's INSTALLER_ARTIFACT line (149-166), runs the REAL staged handshake-native.exe --self-check under clean-profile env_remove(HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT/RUNTIME_ROOT) (231-236), asserts exit 0 + empty missing_assets (252-259), parses the real PE import table via object crate (99-108, 262-270), and writes the report (273-307). Ground-truth: suite GREEN. Not tautological — would fail if staging dropped any asset.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-03: bundled_deps_audit exits 0; report missing:[], disallowed_system_webview:false
acceptance_summary.evidence: bundled_deps_audit.rs walks the real staging tree (list_files_rel), reuses installer::check_bundle_integrity_at as the canonical rule set (line 109), asserts handshake-native.exe + bundled/postgres/pg_ctl.exe + a real font file present (151-153), checks no top-level/bundled WebView2Loader.dll (126-134), writes bundle_deps_audit_report.json (156-171). Ground-truth GREEN.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-04: --self-check exits 0 on clean temp dir with no HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT
acceptance_summary.evidence: smoke runs the staged exe --self-check with env_remove(HANDSHAKE_WORKSPACE_ROOT) and env_remove(HANDSHAKE_RUNTIME_ROOT) (installer_build_smoke.rs:231-236). main.rs handle_cli_flags() runs FIRST in main() (main.rs:54), BEFORE tracing/focus_guard/eframe/PG init, so --self-check is a pure exe-relative path-existence probe (installer/mod.rs:167-176). Verified by direct read of main.rs ordering.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-05: PE import table confirms WebView2Loader.dll absent from system imports
acceptance_summary.evidence: webview2_is_system_import() (installer_build_smoke.rs:98-108) uses object::File::parse + file.imports() against the real staged exe and asserts Some(false) on Windows (262-270). object 0.36 confirmed as a real [dev-dependencies] entry (Cargo.toml:142-154).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-06: staging contains handshake-native.exe, bundled/postgres/pg_ctl(.exe), fonts/ >=1 font, grammars/
acceptance_summary.evidence: build_installer.ps1 stages all four (lines 99-148); REQUIRED_ASSETS in installer/mod.rs:117-129 encodes the same contract; assets/fonts/ contains real Inter-Regular.ttf + Inter-Bold.ttf (verified by directory listing); bundled_deps_audit asserts each present (151-153).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-07: build_installer.ps1 has zero hardcoded absolute paths / drive letters
acceptance_summary.evidence: Independent Grep over `[A-Za-z]:[\/]` against scripts/build_installer.ps1 returned ZERO matches. All paths derive from $PSScriptRoot, $env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR, $env:TEMP, GetPathRoot, Join-Path (ps1:51-69). GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-004 satisfied.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-08: check_bundle_integrity Ok on valid tree, Err(MissingAsset) when asset removed; unit test
acceptance_summary.evidence: installer/mod.rs tests: check_bundle_integrity_returns_ok (line 362, the PT-031-04 named test), check_bundle_integrity_fails_on_missing_postgres (376), _fails_on_empty_fonts_dir (392), _fails_on_missing_grammars_dir (405). Real positive+negative coverage via check_bundle_integrity_at against hand-rolled temp staging trees (no new dep). CTRL-031-05 satisfied.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-031-09: both report JSONs parseable by serde_json
acceptance_summary.evidence: Reports written with serde_json::to_string_pretty (smoke:304, audit:170); self_check_json_shapes_are_valid + self_check_exit_code_tracks_real_bundle_integrity unit tests round-trip the self-check JSON through serde_json::from_str (installer/mod.rs:420-466). Smoke also parses the live self-check JSON (smoke:242-243).

spec_realism.detail: sub-rule 1 (deferred-live escape): independent Grep of installer/mod.rs, main.rs, installer_build_smoke.rs, bundled_deps_audit.rs found NO todo!/unimplemented!/#[ignore] on any MT-031 required-behavior path. The --self-check behavior is fully implemented and runs before any GUI/PG init. sub-rule 2 (real-resource proof): the MT's real-resource claim is the real installer/single-binary build. It IS exercised for real: build_installer.ps1 runs a genuine `cargo build --profile release-native` (crt-static per .cargo/config.toml), the smoke LAUNCHES the resulting real handshake-native.exe (--version and --self-check) and PARSES its real PE import table via the object crate, and Compress-Archive produces a real zip. The only mocked element is the bundled postgres pg_ctl, which the contract EXPLICITLY authorizes to defer (RISK-031-04, RISK-031-08, ps1:130-148 placeholder guidance); it is honestly surfaced as postgres_deferred:true in installer_smoke_report.json rather than hidden. Follow-up commit cb1730b9 makes the exe-relative <exe_dir>/bundled/postgres discovery REAL, unit-tested code in managed_postgres.rs (from_env precedence: HANDSHAKE_MANAGED_PG_BIN > PGBIN > exe-relative bundled > Windows-default/PATH; bundled_bin_dir(exe_dir) anchored on pg_ctl; bundled_bin_dir_some_when_pg_ctl_present test), with the path contract <exe_dir>/bundled/postgres matching installer::BUNDLED_POSTGRES_SUBDIR on all three surfaces (installer staging, runtime self-check, backend discovery). The placeholder is contract-sanctioned scaffolding, not a mock substituting for required behavior. WiX MSI is real .wxs (100 lines, HarvestDirectory) gated off only because the WiX toolchain is absent on this host — the zip is a real single artifact, not a faked .msi.

concerns_non_blocking: NON-BLOCKING (paperwork, per operator directive not a FAIL): MT-031.json lifecycle.status=PENDING and validator_verdict=PENDING; this independent adversarial review IS the certification per the operator directive (no separate validator by design; Spec-Realism sub-rule 3 satisfied by this review).

NON-BLOCKING (path deviation, honestly documented): the contract literally names tests/native_gui/installer_build_smoke.rs and tests/native_gui/bundled_deps_audit.rs, but cargo derives an integration-test target name only from files DIRECTLY under tests/, so they live at tests/installer_build_smoke.rs and tests/bundled_deps_audit.rs. The JSON reports still land in tests/native_gui/artifacts/ as required. Documented in both test headers. Likewise installer/mod.rs lives at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/installer/ (the crate is the build unit) rather than a repo-root src/frontend/installer/; functionally equivalent, within allowed_paths intent.

NON-BLOCKING: the LITERAL AC-031-01 >=10MB-compressed bound remains unmet (~6.47MB) until a host with the real postgres toolchain stages the real managed-postgres binaries. Contract-anticipated (RISK-031-08) and recorded in the report (literal_ac_031_01_met flag, postgres_note). Re-run on a postgres+WiX host to convert the placeholder to real binaries and a real .msi; the literal bound then holds automatically.

NON-BLOCKING (cosmetic .wxs, only matters on a WiX host): handshake_native.wxs sets Package@Version="!(bind.fileVersion.HandshakeNativeExe)" while build_installer.ps1:167-173 passes -d ProductVersion=$productVersion; the ProductVersion bind variable is unused by the Version attribute. Inconsequential because the MSI path is gated off on this host (zip fallback) and the exe FileVersion bind is itself a valid version source. Reconcile before the MSI ships on a WiX-equipped host.

NON-BLOCKING (artifact hygiene, NOT caused by this MT's config): MT-031 build outputs go to a SHORT external CARGO_TARGET_DIR (e.g. <TEMP-drive-root>/hsk-rn), NOT inside the repo tree. Generated *_report.json under tests/native_gui/artifacts/ are gitignored (.gitignore:10). No repo-internal target/ introduced by this MT.

NON-BLOCKING (placeholder pg_ctl content): the deferred pg_ctl.exe is a batch-file placeholder written with a .exe extension (ps1:141-147); the integrity check only asserts .is_file(), so this is sufficient for the bundle-LAYOUT proof the contract scopes here. A production installer MUST replace it with the real managed-postgres binaries (the script already prints this WARNING and the exe-relative discovery code in managed_postgres.rs will consume them).
